content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed both amendments: (1) Added #[cfg(unix)] to all three background-reap tests (managed_child_wait_respects_timeout, background_reap_timeout_drops_handle, spawn_background_reap_calls_real_function) since they use process_group(0) and cat/sleep which are Unix-specific. (2) Added spawn_background_reap_calls_real_function which calls spawn_background_reap_with_timeout directly with a real sleep process and verifies the Arc is dropped, proving the real function works end-to-end. Combined with background_reap_timeout_drops_handle (which deterministically proves the timeout branch works) and managed_child_wait_respects_timeout (which proves timeout bounds wait()), both exit paths of the real function are covered.

## Steps

0. [Completed] Read current test code and identify amendment targets: missing #[cfg(unix)] on tests using process_group/cat, and no test calling spawn_background_reap_with_timeout directly
1. [Completed] Add #[cfg(unix)] to managed_child_wait_respects_timeout and background_reap_timeout_drops_handle to fix non-Unix compilation (process_group, cat are Unix-only)
2. [Completed] Add spawn_background_reap_calls_real_function test: calls spawn_background_reap_with_timeout directly with sleep 300, 5s timeout, verifies Arc dropped within 1s (exercises happy path through real function)
3. [Completed] Verify all three tests pass individually
4. [Completed] Verify full test suite: 585 unit + 756 integration tests passed, 0 failed
5. [Completed] Verify no clippy regressions in process_backend.rs
6. [Completed] Verify cargo fmt --check passes

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test managed_child_wait: 1 passed (deterministic timeout proof)
- cargo test background_reap_timeout: 1 passed (deterministic timeout branch + Arc drop)
- cargo test spawn_background_reap_calls: 1 passed (real function, Arc dropped within 1s)
- cargo test: 585 unit + 756 integration tests passed, 0 failed, 9 doc-tests ignored
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings | grep process_backend: no output
- cargo fmt --check: clean

## Outstanding Risks

- On platforms where SIGKILL is truly ineffective (uninterruptible I/O 'D' state), the process remains as a zombie after handle drop — inherent to the OS; the timeout correctly avoids blocking the tokio runtime
- Pre-existing clippy warnings (16 in other files) remain unfixed — not introduced by this change
